paper_title: Phenological and Morphological Studies of U. indica and U. wightii

methods.description: Recording phenological events such as leaf fall, renewal, flowering, and fruiting periods for U. indica and U. wightii accessions.

steps.description: Phenological events (time of leaf fall, renewal, flowering and fruiting period) were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: record phenological events for plant accessions

methods.description: Conducting morphometrical studies of flowering phenology, including counting the number of flowers and measuring the length of inflorescence, perianth, androecium, and gynoecium.

steps.description: Counting the number of flowers, length of the inflorescence, perianth, androecium and gynoecium of the accessions periodically for about 4 years throughout the flowering period (March to May).
steps.searchable_summary: measure and record morphometrical data of flowers

methods.description: Preparation and examination of pollen slides using light microscopy to measure pollen size.

steps.description: Pollen slides were prepared by mounting pollen grains from freshly dehisced anther suspended in 50% glycerin and mounted on a slide.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen slides for light microscopy

steps.description: Pollen size was measured with an ocular micrometer under light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen size using ocular micrometer

methods.description: Examination of pollen grains using scanning electron microscopy and measurement of pollen morphometrics.

steps.description: Pollen grains were transferred to aluminum stubs and coated with gold.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains for SEM by coating with gold

steps.description: Pollen morphometric measurements were made using Image J software.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen morphometrics using Image J

methods.description: Testing pollen fertility using TTC stain test to determine viable pollen grains.

steps.description: TTC (2,3,5-triphenyl tetrazolium chloride) stain test was used for this purpose.
steps.searchable_summary: perform TTC stain test for pollen fertility

steps.description: The fertile pollen grains stained with TTC dyed magenta color were considered as viable.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TTC stained pollen for viability

steps.description: Pollen fertility was determined as per the formula: Percentage Pollen Fertility = Number of Fertile pollen grain stained / Total number of Pollen grains X 100.

methods.description: Studying pollen germination using Brew Baker's and Kwack culture medium with varying sucrose concentrations.

steps.description: Brew Baker's and Kwack (1963) culture medium with different sucrose concentrations (5%, 15% and 25%) were used for the pollen germination studies.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare culture medium for pollen germination with sucrose
